title: Categorized Free Recall with Open-Loop Stimulation at Encoding
description: This dataset comprises intracranial electrophysiological recordings and behavioral data from a categorized free recall task with open-loop electrical brain stimulation during encoding. Participants studied semantically organized word lists (12 items from 3 categories, 4 words per category) followed by a distractor task and free recall. Stimulation was delivered to hippocampal and entorhinal cortex electrodes during encoding on 20 of 25 lists per session, with detailed stimulation parameters recorded for each trial.
methods_description: Participants performed a categorized free recall task involving visual presentation of word lists, arithmetic distractor problems, and free recall. Intracranial recordings were obtained from multiple clinical sites. Open-loop electrical stimulation was applied to single electrodes in the hippocampus and entorhinal cortex during encoding on alternating word-pair blocks (6 of 12 words per list). Stimulation parameters included anode/cathode configuration, amplitude, pulse frequency, pulse width, and pulse count. Recordings were acquired using Blackrock (250 nV units) and Medtronic (0.1 µV units) systems.
